items.title: CMake project structure: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? Object files built with any compiler flags can be mixed safely as long as the linker accepts their names.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Keep ABI-affecting compiler, library, macro, and language settings consistent across every linked translation unit and dependency.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Keep ABI-affecting compiler, library, macro, and language settings consistent across every linked translation unit and dependency.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Keep ABI-affecting compiler, library, macro, and language settings consistent across every linked translation unit and dependency.
Object files built with any compiler flags can be mixed safely as long as the linker accepts their names.
